_comment: Example replay_filter cuts that SPLIT one DAQ run into full-target and empty-target output files by the target cell pressure (TGT:PRad:Cell_P) — the case where the cell is emptied or filled mid-run without stopping the DAQ. replay_filter classifies every event by its forward-filled Cell_P reading: PV >= 'full' goes to <out>_full.root, PV <= 'empty' goes to <out>_empty.root, and anything in between (the ramp) lands in neither. The split runs on the already-replayed ROOT, so you replay the full run once and separate it in seconds — no second replay. Classification is per-checkpoint and stateless, so any number of full<->empty transitions are handled, and a run that only ever shows one state writes just that one file (a pure full run -> only <out>_full.root; the absent state is skipped). The same config can be run over a whole batch and each run self-sorts. Real PRad-II values: the empty cell reads ~0 and the full cell ~640-1010 (run/energy dependent), so full=500/empty=5 separates the two states robustly across run periods (full MUST be > empty). The dead zone between 'empty' and 'full' is the guard band; set 'guard_checkpoints' > 0 to drop an extra +/- N slow-control points at each state edge for margin beyond the dead zone. labels[0] is the full-target suffix, labels[1] the empty-target suffix (defaults: full / empty). The other blocks (livetime/epics/charge) are the usual quality cuts and apply to BOTH states; delete them for a pure split with no quality filtering.
